_comment: Macro-Terrain: Ancient God Eye Lake. Per scale directive: this terrain IS a being. Player shouldn't initially realize they're standing on bone. Divine Sense -> Perception changes -> 'You realize this mountain range... is one finger.' Prime Directive: the being's corpse is objectively terrain; perception tier determines what the player sees.
name: Ancient God Eye Lake
scale: 10 li diameter, depth immeasurable — one eye of a continent-spanning being.
canonical_event: Tu Si's eye held his last sight before his god-body fell.

perception_tiered_appearance.mortal: A perfectly circular lake 10 li across, water unnaturally clear, depths immeasurable.
perception_tiered_appearance.qi_condensation: The water reflects light oddly; seems to track the viewer.
perception_tiered_appearance.foundation_establishment: The lake is an eye socket; the 'water' is petrified vitreous humor.
perception_tiered_appearance.core_formation: This is an Ancient God's eye; the socket still holds residual god-sight.
perception_tiered_appearance.nascent_soul: The eye holds Tu Si's last sight; those who meet its gaze see what he saw.
perception_tiered_appearance.spirit_severing: The eye is a window to Tu Si's god-memory; cultivating here grants god-knowledge.

perception_mechanic.trigger: When player's perception tier rises, the appearance re-rolls automatically.
perception_mechanic.no_locked_upgrades: A mortal who later reaches Core Formation will see the truth; the terrain does not change, only understanding does.
perception_mechanic.canon_fidelity: This is exactly how Wang Lin perceived Ancient God Tu Si's remains — first as terrain, then as god-bone, then as god-memory.
